title: Graphviz diagram renderer for skill documentation
purpose: Extracts and renders graphviz diagrams from SKILL.md files into SVG format for visualization of process flows.
module_context: This is a command-line script that processes markdown files to extract code blocks and render them as SVG diagrams using graphviz.

methods.name: extractDotBlocks
methods.normalized_params: (markdown: string): Array<{ name: string; content: string }>

steps.content: Find all code blocks in the markdown that are marked as dot language.
Extract the content of each dot block and trim whitespace.
Parse the digraph name from the dot content if present.
Generate a default name for the graph if no name is found in the content.
Return an array of graph objects containing name and content.
Extract the body portion of a digraph by matching the content between opening and closing braces.
Remove any rankdir declarations from the body to avoid conflicts.
Return the cleaned body content with leading whitespace trimmed.

methods.name: combineGraphs
methods.normalized_params: (blocks: Array<{ name: string; content: string }>, skillName: string): string

steps.content: Process each graph block to extract its body content.
Wrap each graph body in a cluster with a labeled subgraph for visual grouping.
Construct a combined digraph with top-level settings for rank direction and clustering.
Return the complete combined dot content as a string.
Execute the graphviz dot command to convert the dot content to SVG format.
IF the conversion succeeds, return the generated SVG content.
IF the conversion fails, log the error and return null.
Parse command-line arguments to determine if diagrams should be combined.
Validate that a skill directory argument was provided.
Resolve the skill directory path and construct the path to SKILL.md.
Verify that the SKILL.md file exists.
Check that the graphviz dot command is available on the system.
Read and parse the markdown content to extract dot blocks.
IF no dot blocks are found, notify the user and exit.
Create the output directory for diagrams if it does not exist.
IF the combine flag is set, merge all diagrams into one and render it.
ELSE render each diagram separately.
